title: Collar and Cuff Sling
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- For the comfort and support of suspected wrist, arm, or shoulder fractures, or soft tissue injuries.

#### Procedure

- If required, administer pain relief prior to applying splint and/or arm sling.

- Confirm distal pulses are present.

- Remove triangular bandage from packaging.

- Have the patient position injured arm so that fingers are resting on/near uninjured side clavicle.

- Roll up the triangular bandage so that each “tying” end is at greatest distance from each other.

- Select one “tying” end and create a loop, with the “tying” end laying over the middle of the bandage, pointing downwards.

- Select the other “tying” end and create a loop, with the “tying” end laying over the middle of the bandage, pointing upwards. The bandage should now resemble a figure eight (see **Figure 1**).

Figure 1

- Place one loop on top of the other so that the bandage resembles a circle with two tails at the base pointing in opposite directions (see **Figure 2**).

Figure 2

- Position the loops over the patient’s injured side wrist and tighten to the appropriate level.

- Secure “tying” ends in place with a knot behind the uninjured side shoulder (see **Figure 3**). 

Figure 3

- Reconfirm distal pulses are present, sensation, and capillary refill.
